raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":1.0,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation directly asks to reframe the conceptual story, making thesis, gap, and contribution reconstruction load-bearing.","R1.2":"A revised conceptual story must preserve a coherent argument from benchmark construction to judge validation to pipeline evaluation.","R1.3":"The artifact’s central claims depend on reported agreement scores and benchmark results, so claims must remain aligned with those reported findings.","R1.5":"Reframing the story centrally involves the section-level narrative arc connecting benchmark need, method, findings, and implications.","RX.1":"Uncertainty must be disclosed because the artifact makes inferential moves from observed agreement to broader clinical utility.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is critical because the artifact extends from benchmark grading to drop-in clinical replacement and automated ACMG/AMP classification.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is active because the task is only to project responsibility for reframing, not to rewrite or evaluate the full paper.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact concerns clinical variant interpretation and diagnostic laboratory reporting.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are needed to distinguish what is grounded in the artifact from responsibility inferred from the delegation."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation directly asks to reframe the conceptual story, making thesis, gap, and contribution reconstruction load-bearing.
rationale.R1.2: A revised conceptual story must preserve a coherent argument from benchmark construction to judge validation to pipeline evaluation.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact’s central claims depend on reported agreement scores and benchmark results, so claims must remain aligned with those reported findings.
rationale.R1.5: Reframing the story centrally involves the section-level narrative arc connecting benchmark need, method, findings, and implications.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty must be disclosed because the artifact makes inferential moves from observed agreement to broader clinical utility.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is critical because the artifact extends from benchmark grading to drop-in clinical replacement and automated ACMG/AMP classification.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is active because the task is only to project responsibility for reframing, not to rewrite or evaluate the full paper.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is load-bearing because the artifact concerns clinical variant interpretation and diagnostic laboratory reporting.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are needed to distinguish what is grounded in the artifact from responsibility inferred from the delegation.
